title: ophrys × breuerorum
intro: Ophrys × breuerorum, also known as Breuer's bee orchid, is a hybrid orchid species that belongs to the genus Ophrys and is characterized by its unique appearance, mimicking female bees to attract pollinators. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its alkaloid content. The health benefits of Ophrys × breuerorum include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
